title: 10 best herbal essential oils for numbness in hands
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for numbness in hands are plant-based extracts used to alleviate numbness and discomfort in the hands. These oils work by improving blood circulation, reducing inflammation, and providing pain relief, making them a natural remedy for numbness. The best herbal essential oils for numbness in hands include Zingiber officinale, Capsicum annuum, Eucalyptus globulus, Cinnamomum verum, Mentha x piperita, Piper nigrum, Rosmarinus officinalis, Elettaria cardamomum, Lavandula angustifolia, and Ginkgo biloba. If left untreated, numbness in hands can hinder daily activities, cause emotional distress, and even lead to permanent damage, affecting one's quality of life and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary essential oils helps with numbness in hands because it increases blood flow to the extremities, reducing the likelihood of numbness caused by poor circulation. The oil's ability to stimulate the brain and nervous system also helps to improve sensation and reduce tingling sensations in the fingers and toes. Additionally, rosemary's anti-inflammatory properties can help alleviate any underlying conditions that may be contributing to numbness, such as arthritis or carpal tunnel syndrome, promoting overall hand health and functionality.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Rosemary essential oil's antioxidant properties help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in rosemary essential oil can help to reduce swelling and pain in the hands and wrists, which can alleviate numbness and tingling sensations.

Circulatory stimulant properties: Rosemary essential oil's ability to stimulate blood circulation and improve oxygenation of the tissues can help to increase sensation and alleviate numbness and tingling in the hands.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots are good for making essential oils to relieve numbness in hands because they contain compounds that help improve circulation and reduce inflammation.
Seeds: Rosemary seeds are used to make essential oils that can help alleviate numbness in hands by promoting blood flow and reducing stiffness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality rosmarinus officinalis leaves from organic farms or your own garden in the early morning after dew has dried.
Dry the freshly harvested leaves using a dehydrator at 40°c for 24 hours or air dry them for several days until crumbly.
Steam distill 1 cup of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves with 4 cups of distilled water for 30 minutes to obtain essential oil.
Filter the resulting distillate through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into dark glass bottles and store in a cool place for future use.
Add 10 drops of the freshly prepared essential oil to a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil and massage onto affected hands three times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass essential oils helps with numbness in hands because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically, lemongrass oil can stimulate blood flow to the affected area, reducing swelling and alleviating numbness caused by poor circulation or nerve damage. Additionally, its citrusy scent can help to uplift and calm the mind, reducing stress and anxiety that may be contributing to the numbness. By promoting relaxation and relieving discomfort, lemongrass essential oil can provide natural relief from numb hands.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: Lemongrass essential oil has antispasmodic properties, which can help to relax muscles and reduce spasms, alleviating numbness caused by muscle cramps or strain.
Analgesic: The analgesic properties of lemongrass essential oil can help to numb the pain associated with numbness in hands, providing temporary relief from discomfort and inflammation.
Antioxidant: Lemongrass essential oil has antioxidant properties that can help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which may contribute to numbness in hands and fingers.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes, also known as underground stems, contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds that help to stimulate blood circulation and relieve numbness in the hands.
Barks: The barks of lemongrass plants are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.
remedy_recipe: Gather 500g of dried lemongrass leaves and stems and transfer them into a clean glass jar for later use.
Steam distill the dried lemongrass material using a steam distillation apparatus at atmospheric pressure for 3 hours.
Collect the essential oil that separates from the water as it cools and transfers it to a clean dark glass bottle for storage.
Mix 10ml of the cymbopogon citratus essential oil with 100ml of jojoba carrier oil in a small bowl and stir well until combined.
Store the finished blend in an airtight container at room temperature away from direct sunlight for up to 6 months.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried salvia officinalis leaves and stems from the mediterranean region.
Cleanse the plant material by gently blowing away any debris and insects.
Steam distill the plant material using 30g of herb and 150ml of water for 20 minutes.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove impurities.
Store the salvia officinalis essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wintergreen essential oils helps with numbness in hands because of its ability to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh gaultheria procumbens leaves and stems measuring approximately one pound.
Dry the harvested plant material using air circulation or desiccant for eight hours.
Steam distill the dried gaultheria procumbens plant material with water at a ratio of 0.25 pounds to one quart for three hours.
Collect and filter the extracted essential oil through activated charcoal and cotton wool within ten minutes.
Bottle and store the final essential oil in dark glass containers, allowing it to mature for seven days.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves from a healthy plant at dawn when essential oil content is highest.
Dry the freshly harvested leaves in a warm oven at 60 degrees fahrenheit for 4 hours or until brittle.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder and transfer to a clean glass jar.
Steam distill the ground melissa officinalis leaves using a steam distillation apparatus with water and condenser setup at atmospheric pressure.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth and store in dark amber glass bottles away from direct sunlight for later use.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eucalyptus leaves from a healthy plant, collecting at least 200g of leaves for distillation.
Wash the harvested leaves gently with cold water to remove any dirt or debris, then pat dry with a paper towel.
Steam distill the dried eucalyptus leaves using a traditional still, monitoring the temperature and pressure to ensure optimal conditions, taking approximately 4-6 hours.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove any impurities or contaminants, retaining only the clear liquid gold.
Bottle the final product in dark glass containers, storing at room temperature away from direct sunlight for up to 12 months while maintaining its therapeutic potency and aroma.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh leaves of pimenta racemosa plant weighing approximately 500 grams.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 8 hours or until brittle.
Crush the dried leaves into fine powder using mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Steam distill the powdered leaves in a clevenger apparatus with 100ml of water at atmospheric pressure for 2 hours.
Filter the resulting essential oil through cheesecloth into dark glass bottle for storage and use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with numbness in hands because of its invigorating and refreshing properties. The menthol content in peppermint oil increases blood circulation, which helps to relieve numbness, tingling, and stiffness in the fingers and hands. remedy_properties: Pain-relieving properties: Peppermint essential oil's analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce pain and numbness in the hands by blocking the production of prostaglandins, which are chemical messengers that transmit pain signals.

Circulation-boosting properties: Peppermint essential oil's vasodilatory properties help to increase blood flow to the hands, which can help to alleviate numbness caused by poor circulation or Raynaud's disease.

Anti-spasmodic properties: Peppermint essential oil's ability to relax muscles and reduce spasms helps to relieve tension and numbness in the hands, making it an effective natural remedy for conditions like carpal tunnel syndrome.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from your garden or local market, weighing approximately 100 grams.
Dry the harvested peppermint leaves using a warm air circulation oven at 60 degrees celsius for two hours.
Steam distill the dried peppermint leaves using a stainless steel distillation apparatus with 500 milliliters of water for three hours.
Collect the resulting essential oil from the condenser of the distillation apparatus, yielding approximately 10 milliliters.
Filter the collected essential oil through a sterile cotton cloth or paper filter to remove any impurities and sediment.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 fresh origanum vulgare leaves and rinse them gently with distilled water to remove any impurities.
Combine the leaves with 500ml of high-proof ethanol in a glass jar and seal it tightly, allowing for a 4-hour maceration period.
Steam distill the mixture using a clevenger apparatus at atmospheric pressure and a temperature range of 100-150°f for 2 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the resulting hydrosol by centrifugation or decantation and store it in a dark glass bottle at room temperature.
Dilute the essential oil with a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at a 3% concentration ratio, shaking well before applying topically to affected areas for relief from numbness.
